response.value: I’ll replace the current Dockerfile with a complete multi-stage build that compiles your Go API and runs it in a minimal runtime image. I’m quickly checking the app entrypoint and exposed port first so the container starts correctly.
I found the server setup: it listens on Gin default port 8080 and serves /health. Next I’m writing a production-ready Dockerfile with dependency caching and a lightweight runtime image.

edits.text:
FROM golang:1.26.1 AS builder

WORKDIR /src

COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download

COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -o /app/health-api .

FROM alpine:3.22

WORKDIR /app

RUN adduser -D -H appuser

COPY --from=builder /app/health-api /app/health-api

EXPOSE 8080

USER appuser

ENTRYPOINT ["/app/health-api"]

response.value: I’ve updated the file and now I’m validating it by attempting a Docker build so we can catch any syntax or runtime-image issues immediately.

parts.text: why does this app return the timestamp in UTC if run in docker, it display the correct timezone (WIB) if i run it locally

message.text: why does this app return the timestamp in UTC if run in docker, it display the correct timezone (WIB) if i run it locally

response.value: You’re seeing a common container behavior: the app inside Docker is likely using the container’s default timezone (UTC), not your host timezone. I’ll inspect the time handling and then patch the Dockerfile so it uses WIB consistently.

and formats it with local timezone, so Docker defaults to UTC unless you configure the container timezone. I’m applying two small fixes now: set container TZ to Asia/Jakarta and make timestamp formatting explicitly use Asia/Jakarta so it stays correct even if runtime TZ changes.

edits.text:
FROM golang:1.26.1

WORKDIR /usr/src/app

RUN apt-get update \
	&& apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends tzdata \
	&& rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

ENV TZ=Asia/Jakarta
RUN ln -snf /usr/share/zoneinfo/$TZ /etc/localtime && echo $TZ > /etc/timezone

COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download

COPY . .
RUN go build -v -o /usr/local/bin/health-api ./

ENV GIN_MODE=release
EXPOSE 8080

CMD ["health-api"]

response.value: I’ve applied the timezone fixes in both app code and container config; next I’ll run a quick local Go check and a Docker build to verify everything works end-to-end.
